title: Fix compatibility matrix release evidence prerequisites
outOfScope: Changing the release tag or published v0.5.24 evidence

sources.reason: Failing PR compatibility jobs and their checkout/toolchain setup.

riskAssessment.reason: The fix only makes existing compatibility prerequisites explicit and preserves the matrix contract.

executionDecision.reason: Observed failures are directly within the declared compatibility workflow scope.

preReviewWarnings: Review checkout tag availability and explicit Rust toolchain behavior.

checkpointPolicy.reason: Record at least one checkpoint before finishing to reduce mid-task drift.

acceptance: Compatibility checkout depth is zero so release-evidence tag resolution is available.
Rust compatibility setup passes an explicit stable toolchain to the pinned action.
tests/test_workflows.py asserts checkout depth zero and explicit Rust stable configuration.
make project-test and the required AI Work Item finish checks pass after the workflow change.
guidelines: CI prerequisites must be explicit in the workflow configuration.
Workflow changes must preserve pinned Action SHAs.

intent.problem: The newly required compatibility gate exposed two latent CI assumptions: shallow checkout hides the historical release tag and the pinned Rust toolchain action now requires an explicit toolchain input.
constraints: Preserve all existing pinned Action SHAs.
Do not alter published release metadata or historical tags.
Keep the compatibility matrix job names stable for branch protection.
intent.rationale: Making both prerequisites explicit restores deterministic cross-platform evidence for the required compatibility gate.

restrictedWriteApproval.reason: User explicitly authorized continuation and accepted the PR-first CI remediation.

rollbackNote: Revert this Work Item diff and restore related tests and docs.
